title: podman import syntax fix
body:
syntax updated for podman import --change
    
currently, podman import change do not support syntax like
- KEY val
- KEY ["val"]
This adds support for both of these syntax along with KEY=val
Fixes #4000     

Signed-off-by: Kunal Kushwaha <kunal.kushwaha@gmail.com>

CMD and Entrypoints both supports string array as expected with this update.

Yeah, those look like flakes we fixed upstream
I re-run the test, I guess that results into all green :)
Could we get an example or two added to the man page?
Hi @vrothberg  I have added unit test for `ParseChages`.  PTAL

have added these testcases.

I think `parseChanges()` works as expected as of now. Though It can be improved with various implementations and we can do it in next PR as discussed earlier. We can add more testcases too along with that. 
I would request to merge this PR if no serious issues pending against it.
Lets get this merged, waiting for @vrothberg approval.

@rhatdan @baude @mheon @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL

Note that the parsing is not yet 100 percent accurate (see an example below) but a huge improvement over the current state. 
```go
// CMD ["/bin/sh","arg-with,comma"]
_, vals, err = ParseChanges(`CMD ["/bin/sh","arg-with,comma"]`)
assert.EqualValues(t, []string{`"/bin/sh"`, `"arg-with`, `comma"`}, vals)
assert.NoError(t, err)
```
LGTM, but would like @mheon to take one more look before merging.
